title: Release flow: version stamping, DMG artifact, Homebrew tap

description: Stamp version/build into Info.plist in build-app.sh
details: Edit `Scripts/build-app.sh`. After copying `Resources/Info.plist` into the bundle and BEFORE the codesign step, add: if `VERSION` env var is set, `/usr/libexec/PlistBuddy -c "Set :CFBundleShortVersionString $VERSION" "$CONTENTS_DIR/Info.plist"`; if `BUILD_NUMBER` is set, same for `:CFBundleVersion`. Default behavior with neither set must be unchanged (plist copied verbatim) so local dev builds keep working. Echo the stamped version in the final output. Verification: `VERSION=9.9.9 BUILD_NUMBER=42 ./Scripts/build-app.sh && defaults read "$PWD/.build/Taskbarra.app/Contents/Info" CFBundleShortVersionString` prints `9.9.9`, and `codesign --verify .build/Taskbarra.app` passes. Plain `./Scripts/build-app.sh` still prints `0.1.0`.
notes: Updated Scripts/build-app.sh to stamp VERSION and BUILD_NUMBER into the copied bundle plist before codesign and report the effective version. Verified stamped 9.9.9/42, ad-hoc signature validity, and unchanged default 0.1.0 behavior.

details: New executable `Scripts/package-dmg.sh` (bash, `set -euo pipefail`, same ROOT_DIR pattern as build-app.sh). Inputs: `VERSION` env (required), app at `.build/Taskbarra.app` (fail with a clear message if missing). Steps: create staging dir `.build/dmg-staging`, `ditto` the app in, `ln -s /Applications` inside staging, `hdiutil create -volname Taskbarra -srcfolder .build/dmg-staging -ov -format UDZO .build/Taskbarra-$VERSION.dmg`, clean staging, print the dmg path and its `shasum -a 256`. No create-dmg or other deps. Verification: after t-001's build, `VERSION=9.9.9 ./Scripts/package-dmg.sh` produces `.build/Taskbarra-9.9.9.dmg`; `hdiutil attach` shows `Taskbarra.app` and an `Applications` symlink; detach cleanly.
notes: Added executable Scripts/package-dmg.sh using hdiutil UDZO, ditto, an Applications symlink, staging cleanup, and SHA-256 output. Verified the test DMG mounts with Taskbarra.app + symlink, embeds version/build 9.9.9/42, detaches, and cleans staging.

description: Rework release-build.yml: stamped build, DMG upload
details: Rewrite `.github/workflows/release-build.yml` (keep trigger `release: published`, runner `macos-14`, Xcode select step). Derive `VERSION=${TAG#v}` from `github.event.release.tag_name` and `BUILD_NUMBER=${{ github.run_number }}`. Run `CONFIGURATION=release TASKBARRA_CODESIGN_IDENTITY=- VERSION=... BUILD_NUMBER=... ./Scripts/build-app.sh`, then `VERSION=... ./Scripts/package-dmg.sh`. Upload `Taskbarra-$VERSION.dmg` with `gh release upload "$TAG" ... --clobber`; drop the zip artifact step. Precondition: none — the `Taskbarra-*-macos-ad-hoc.zip` artifact name is referenced only inside `release-build.yml` itself (verified via `rg -l "macos-ad-hoc|\.zip"` across the repo), no published release currently carries a zip asset, and no cask/doc links to it. Compute `sha256` of the dmg and expose it as job outputs `dmg_sha256` and `version` for t-005's cask-bump job. Verification: `actionlint .github/workflows/release-build.yml` passes (or careful manual YAML review if actionlint unavailable); full E2E deferred to the next real release.
notes: Reworked release-build.yml to validate vX.Y.Z tags, stamp tag version + GitHub run build number, package/upload Taskbarra-X.Y.Z.dmg, and expose version/SHA-256 job outputs. actionlint passed; no zip/ad-hoc artifact references remain.

description: Create homebrew-tap repo with initial taskbarra cask
details: Create public repo `jalbarrang/homebrew-tap` (`gh repo create jalbarrang/homebrew-tap --public --description "Homebrew tap for jalbarrang's tools"`). If it already exists, just add the cask. Add `Casks/taskbarra.rb`: `cask "taskbarra"` with `version` (current latest release, no `v` prefix), `sha256` (compute from that release's DMG once t-003 has produced one; use `:no_check` placeholder ONLY if no DMG release exists yet), `url "https://github.com/jalbarrang/taskbarra/releases/download/v#{version}/Taskbarra-#{version}.dmg"`, `name`, `desc "Windows-style per-window taskbar for macOS"`, `homepage`, `app "Taskbarra.app"`, `depends_on macos: ">= :sonoma"`, and a `caveats` block stating: app is ad-hoc signed — install with `--no-quarantine` or run `xattr -dr com.apple.quarantine /Applications/Taskbarra.app`; Accessibility permission must be re-granted after every update. Add a minimal README listing available casks with the `brew install --cask jalbarrang/tap/taskbarra --no-quarantine` one-liner, written so future formulae/casks (pit, hiker, …) can be appended. Verification: `brew tap jalbarrang/tap && brew style --cask taskbarra` (or `brew audit --cask taskbarra`) reports no errors. STOP if `gh auth` cannot create repos — ask the user.
notes: Created and pushed public jalbarrang/homebrew-tap with Casks/taskbarra.rb and generic README. Current v0.2.0 has no DMG, so cask intentionally uses sha256 :no_check until the next release. Updated to Homebrew 6 DSL after lint findings; brew style reports no offenses and brew info resolves macOS >=14 plus caveats.

description: CI job to auto-bump cask on release
details: Append a second job `bump-cask` to `.github/workflows/release-build.yml`, `needs: macos-app`, `runs-on: ubuntu-latest`. Steps: checkout `jalbarrang/homebrew-tap` using `token: ${{ secrets.TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN }}`; script-replace the `version` and `sha256` lines in `Casks/taskbarra.rb` using the `version` and `dmg_sha256` outputs from the build job; commit as github-actions bot with message `taskbarra <version>` and push. Make the job fail loudly (not silently skip) if the secret is empty. Document the required secret in the workflow header comment: fine-grained PAT, repo `homebrew-tap`, contents: read/write, saved as `TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN` in taskbarra repo secrets. STOP: if the user has not created `TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN`, ask them to before declaring this task verified. Verification: actionlint passes; on next release the tap repo receives a commit updating both fields.
notes: Added cross-repo bump-cask job with explicit secret validation, tap checkout, strict version/SHA replacement, bot commit, and push. actionlint and replacement dry run pass. User confirmed TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN is saved; current gh CLI token cannot list Actions secrets (403), so full push verification remains the next real release.

details: Update `README.md` with an Installation section: (a) Homebrew: `brew install --cask jalbarrang/tap/taskbarra --no-quarantine`; (b) manual DMG: download, drag to Applications, `xattr -dr com.apple.quarantine /Applications/Taskbarra.app`. Add an Updating subsection: ad-hoc signature changes each release → macOS asks to re-grant Accessibility after updating; brief steps to re-grant. Update the Distribución row in `CONTEXT.md` from "DMG directo + Sparkle auto-update + Homebrew cask" to reflect reality: DMG ad-hoc + Homebrew tap, Sparkle/notarization deliberately deferred (keep CONTEXT.md in Spanish, one-line rationale). Do not hard-wrap prose. Verification: README renders correctly; grep confirms no remaining claim that Sparkle is currently integrated.
notes: Updated README with Homebrew 6-compatible install/update commands, manual DMG path, quarantine removal, and Accessibility re-grant steps; updated CONTEXT to reflect ad-hoc DMG + generic tap and deferred Sparkle/notarization. Corrected and pushed matching tap README/caveats. Homebrew removed --no-quarantine, so implementation deliberately uses explicit xattr instead.

details: Fix sorted imports in NotificationDeepLinkPolicyTests.swift, two >120-character lines in NotificationPrivacyFilterTests.swift, and trailing newline count in NotificationDeepLinkExtractorTests.swift. Then run ./Scripts/check.sh.
notes: Final ./Scripts/check.sh reaches SwiftLint but fails on four untouched test-file violations, preventing the repository's full quality gate from passing independently of the release-flow changes.
